formatted_address: 6310 Laguna Bay Ct, Houston, TX 77041, USA
pitch_rationale: The homes at this Houston, TX location feature multi-faceted hip roofs with no prominent gables or dormers. Shadow lengths cast from the eaves onto surrounding hardscape measure approximately 8–12 feet using the 50-ft scale bar, consistent with a moderate ~5:12 pitch given estimated eave heights of 12–14 ft. The visible roof planes are broad relative to the overall footprint but show clear tonal gradation between faces, ruling out a very shallow 3:12–4:12 or a steep 7:12+ pitch. The southern Houston climate strongly favors 4:12–6:12 pitches, and the roof-to-eave geometry with ridges sitting moderately inward from the eave perimeter best matches a 5:12 estimate.
footprint_rationale: The orange bounding box spans approximately 380 px (wide) × 342 px (tall), equating to 80.7 ft × 72.6 ft = 5,859 sq ft bounding area. The subject is a large, complex multi-section hip roof with no visible gable ends (fully hipped). The pool in the upper-right of the bounding box and paved patio/driveway areas in the lower-left and lower portions are excluded from the roof footprint. A fill factor of ~0.68 was applied to account for these non-roof areas, yielding ~3,984 sq ft. Roof edges were traced section by section: numerous short ridge lines sit atop each hip section (~39 ft total); hip lines are abundant at every corner and section intersection (~144 ft); valleys appear at the re-entrant angles where sections join (~28 ft); rake edges are minimal given the fully hipped design (~12 ft); eave lines run along the full perimeter of all sections (~212 ft). Confidence is moderate (0.45) due to the complex multi-plane geometry, oblique view angle, and partial shadowing making individual edge identification challenging.
